notes: Stage 1 long-run signoff manifest. These rows are scoreable only under tools/run_benchmarks.py --run-class signoff, with endpoint identity intact, legacy loop-buffer activity zero, canonical standalone decoded-op replay telemetry present and zero, and no standalone UOC, same-tail, or local lookahead replay plusargs. counter_invariants are checked on every run_class (signoff and dse); counter_targets_stage1 are checked only on signoff.

schema_extensions.counter_invariants: Per-counter {min,max} bounds that MUST hold on every run regardless of class. Use to lock in structural invariants (e.g. owner-identity counters that must remain zero post-FTQ-split).
schema_extensions.counter_targets_stage1: Per-counter {min,max} bounds enforced only when --run-class signoff. Use to encode Stage 1 acceptance gates beyond flags+checksum so the doc-level pass condition is machine-checkable.
schema_extensions.golden_pc_path: Optional. Path to a $readmemh-format file of expected commit PCs (one 16-hex-digit PC per line). When present and run_benchmarks.py forwards +CHECK_GOLDEN_PCS, the testbench asserts every retired PC matches the golden stream and finishes with [GOLDEN_PC TRIP] on first divergence.

benchmarks.name: dhrystone_300_stage1_anchor
benchmarks.notes: Stage 1 Dhrystone-300 apples-to-apples anchor image; hash-gated to prevent stale-image drift. counter_targets_stage1 left empty; populate when Stage 1 RTL work starts converging on numerical bounds (proposed: dup_last_emit max 5000, packet_empty_noemit_dup max 3000, ftq_occ_max min 4).

benchmarks.name: coremark_iter10_checkedin
benchmarks.notes: Heavy CoreMark signoff row. CoreMark/MHz is scoreable only with checksum 64687 and flags=0. counter_targets_stage1 left empty; populate when Stage 1 RTL work starts converging (proposed: dup_last_emit max 50000, packet_empty_noemit_dup max 50000, f2_owner_no_head max 5000, ftq_occ_max min 4, packet_buf_occ_max min 2).
